compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: E1 DESCOPE NOTE (2026-07-16): E1's finding-adjudication TP/FP corpus was INVALIDATED (tool-less, plan-truncated labeling that doesn't match the whole-plan/code-grounded/agentic production gate; ~79 percent contaminated) and DESCOPED — the independent human-adjudicated gold set is parked as idea e59d-c078-724d-46e5. E1 still delivers the VALID outcome corpus + finding sample. This story (R1) relies on E1's §5 re-verification (the 3 review-miss cases dc58/db7b/5886), the OUTCOME-corpus method — UNAFFECTED by the adjudication-corpus invalidation. Gated on E2+E3. A future independent FP/TP gold set (the idea) would strengthen its threshold calibration.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The E1-verified miss class (epic 6982, §5 of docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md;
runs/README.md §"3 MISSED asserted-capability cases") is: **a plan asserts that an existing,
NAMED module provides (or lacks) a capability, and the assertion is false against the code** —
so the executing agent builds on a capability that isn't there, or rebuilds one that already
is. The three verified misses:

- `dc58` — the plan's sign step used a helper `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` does not have
  (signing lives in `rebar.signing`; completion.py:350 only *references* the close gate's
  signing). Pipeline was redesigned mid-flight.
- `db7b` — the plan proposed bounding the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar via event **compaction**, but
  by rebar's own contract compaction folds ONLY `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES` (`_commands/compact.py:206`)
  and REVIEW_RESULT is reducer-ignored, so compaction never absorbs it. Retention is instead a
  dedicated `sidecar.prune` (`llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:72`).
- `5886` — the plan proposed a NEW unmapped-Jira-status alert that `fetcher.py` already emits
  (`_flag_unmapped_statuses` → `fetcher-unmapped-jira-status`, fetcher.py:219). The bug was
  gate-exempt, so nobody looked.

E4 (assumption/premise verification, blocking) probes assertions broadly but MISSED all three:
the named module EXISTS, so E4's existence check passes; the defect is the finer-grained
**capability-surface** mismatch (module present, specific capability absent — or present when
the plan says absent). This story ships a focused probe for exactly that surface.

## What

Add one new **advisory, code-grounded, AGENT-tier** plan-review criterion, id
`asserted-capability`, in the G6/E4 rubric family (facet `codebase-grounding`), that fires when
a plan makes a **capability-surface claim about a named module/symbol that it relies on**, and
that claim is refuted by the code. Two symmetric directions (one concern — capability claims
about named modules must be grounded):

- (A) plan asserts named module M PROVIDES capability C, but M lacks C (dc58, db7b);
- (B) plan asserts capability C is ABSENT / must be built, but M already provides C (5886).

**Mechanism (grounded against `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`).** The probe plugs into the EXISTING
generic pass machinery — no core pass change. Pass-1 runs it as one agentic finder
(`passes.pass1_chunk(..., agentic=True)`, dispatched because its exec tier is `AGENT`;
eval_solver.py:112-113 and pass1.py:3 "one agent per code-grounding criterion"). The finder
greps/reads the repo (and `resolve_symbol` for installed deps) to confirm the module's actual
capability surface. Pass-2 verifier grades its two criterion-local binary sub-answers; Pass-3
computes validity×impact deterministically; Pass-4 coaches surviving advisories. It ships
**advisory posture (never blocks)** — registry entry `default_posture: "advisory"`.

**Registration seam.** Because it is a packaged built-in LLM criterion it must be added to
`registry.CANONICAL_LLM` (registry.py:79), `CODEBASE_GROUNDED` (:66), and `AGENT_TIER` (:72),
given a routing entry in `criteria_routing.json`, a rubric prompt at
`reviewers/plan_review_asserted_capability.md`, and a regenerated `## asserted-capability`
guide section — else `registry.validate_packaged_routing()` (:430) flags it ORPHAN and CI fails
(the exact failure that bit R2/b080), and `validate_criteria_guide()` (:530) flags a missing
section.

**Prior art.** This extends the SAME four-pass finder/verifier/decide/coach machinery already
used by A1/E4/G6/removal-rationale (see docs/plan-review-gate.md); `removal-rationale` (the
removal-side code-grounded advisory dual of A1) is the direct structural precedent for a
gated-then-grounded two-sub-answer code criterion. No new pass, no new engine construct.

**Effectiveness measurement is future dogfood, not a blocking AC.** The just-landed standing
recorder `criterion_effectiveness.py` (commit 0d695be47, d8a5) AUTO-INCLUDES every criterion id
it sees, so `asserted-capability` accrues `detection_proxy` / `blocking_fp_proxy` from real
reviews with ZERO per-criterion wiring. The advisory→blocking promotion gate is the protocol
d8a5 documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md §"Standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder +
advisory→blocking promotion gate"; this story cites it, it is not gated on accumulation time.

## Scope

- IN: the new criterion (registry + routing + rubric + guide), deterministic unit tests, a
  committed `criteria eval` fixture set + its result artifact (the bounded sanity check).
- OUT: promotion to blocking (future dogfood-gated `criteria_routing.json` change per the cited
  promotion gate); any E2/E3 batch eval (permanently rejected on cost); changing E4/A1.
- Files: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py`, `.../criteria_routing.json`,
  `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_asserted_capability.md`,
  `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` (regenerated),
  `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-asserted-capability.eval.yaml`,
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/asserted_capability_sanity.json`,
  `tests/unit/test_asserted_capability_criterion.py`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The criterion is REGISTERED advisory and non-orphan. Proof:
  `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review import registry as r; assert 'asserted-capability' in r.CANONICAL_LLM; assert 'asserted-capability' in r.CODEBASE_GROUNDED and 'asserted-capability' in r.AGENT_TIER; assert r.validate_packaged_routing()==[]; import json; e=json.load(open('src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json'))['asserted-capability']; assert e['default_posture']=='advisory' and e['exec']=='AGENT' and e['facet']=='codebase-grounding'; print('OK')"`
  prints `OK`.
- [ ] The rubric prompt loads and the guide has its section. Proof:
  `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review import registry as r; assert '## asserted-capability' in open('docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md').read(); print(r.explain_criterion('asserted-capability')[:40])"`
  prints a non-empty section head; and `rebar explain asserted-capability` exits 0.
- [ ] Deterministic unit tests for the non-LLM logic (registration invariants, routing/finding
  shape, prompt-contract sub-answer keys) pass. Proof:
  `pytest tests/unit/test_asserted_capability_criterion.py -q` exits 0.
- [ ] Bounded sanity check committed: a `criteria eval` fixture set with the 3 E1-verified misses
  (dc58/db7b/5886, reconstructed to their original false-asserting plan text, grounded against
  current modules) as must-fire and 3 clean plans as must-not-fire. The committed result
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/asserted_capability_sanity.json` shows the probe FIRES
  on >=2 of 3 misses (report §6 R1 threshold; observed 3/3, stability 1.0) and that any clean-plan
  fire is ADVISORY (never blocks) and is recorded as the promotion-gate FP signal. Proof: the JSON
  records `recall_over_misses>=0.66` (observed 1.0), preserves the raw per-case
  `rebar criteria eval asserted-capability --output json` output, and records
  `false_accept_over_clean` (observed 0.33 = one advisory false-fire on the no-claim control N1,
  a non-blocking dogfood datapoint given advisory posture — the exact signal the advisory→blocking
  promotion gate exists to watch). (6 live single-criterion runs total; NO E2/E3 corpus, NO batch
  eval.)
- [ ] The criterion auto-flows into the standing recorder. Proof:
  `python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/criterion_effectiveness.py --report` (or a
  targeted unit assertion on `compute_effectiveness`) yields output in which
  `asserted-capability` appears once it has a firing — verified by a unit test feeding one
  synthetic firing row through `firings_from_review`/`compute_effectiveness` and asserting the
  id is auto-included (zero per-criterion wiring).
- [ ] The advisory→blocking promotion gate is documented and cited: docs/plan-review-gate.md's
  §"Standing per-criterion effectiveness recorder + advisory→blocking promotion gate" (added by
  d8a5) is the authority; this criterion's rubric/guide state it ships advisory and promotion is
  a future dogfood-gated `criteria_routing.json` change. Proof: `grep -n "advisory" ` on the
  rubric shows the advisory posture note.
- [ ] [operator-attested] Landed through Gerrit: change carries `LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`
  and is submitted to main; `make lint && make typecheck && make test` are green on the change.
  Evidence recorded on this ticket (change number, merge commit, vote outcomes) — verified by
  attestation, not an in-session repo check.
compiled_state.title: R1: asserted-capability grounding probe (G6/E4 rubric family, gated on E2+E3)
